hero_subtitle: SFTY organizes Travel Rule data, message routing, exception handling, and case management within a unified workflow designed specifically for crypto-related payment providers.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Travel Rule data remains disconnected across workflows
problem_description: Payment providers struggle to consolidate originator, beneficiary, routing status and exception information within one operational flow. This fragmentation slows case handling, weakens audit evidence and complicates compliance review across crypto-related payment operations.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY integrates originator and beneficiary details, message status, exceptions and wallet activity into one structured workflow that supports case management and audit evidence for payment providers.
workflow_title: From Travel Rule exceptions to case evidence
workflow_description: SFTY captures Travel Rule message exceptions, routes them through case workflows, preserves originator and beneficiary data, and prepares audit evidence for compliance review.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Not another Travel Rule provider. The workflow layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects Travel Rule messages, exceptions, cases and evidence into one compliance layer without replacing existing providers or isolating critical operational data.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports payment providers managing Travel Rule data with audit-ready evidence, provider orchestration and secure deployment for clearer governance and due diligence.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support Travel Rule workflows for payment providers
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how Travel Rule messages route and update status
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how audit evidence is preserved for Travel Rule cases
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe exception handling processes within Travel Rule compliance
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for payment providers?
faq_1_answer: SFTY helps payment providers structure Travel Rule messaging, routing, status tracking, originator and beneficiary data into one operational compliance workflow connected to other digital asset controls.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage Travel Rule exceptions?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports exception handling by creating cases for failed or incomplete Travel Rule messages, routing them for manual review and tracking resolution within a single compliance platform.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve Travel Rule evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-ready evidence by recording Travel Rule message history, related customer data, review decisions, and case files to support defensible compliance and internal audits.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with Travel Rule providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates outputs from multiple Travel Rule providers and connects their data streams into connected workflows without disrupting existing provider infrastructure.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing Travel Rule providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above Travel Rule providers and does not replace them; it structures their outputs into compliance workflows, case management, evidence packages and reporting.
final_title: Connect Travel Rule data into controlled workflows
final_description: Review how SFTY structures Travel Rule messages, routing, exceptions and cases into one compliance operating layer.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
